profile_summary: An 18-year-old right-sided attacker who already operates as one of the most productive young wide players in Europe, combining elite dribbling with a mature passing range.
strengths: Exceptional 1v1 dribbling and close control in tight spaces
High chance creation output (xAG) for his age
Composed decision-making under pressure on the ball
Capable of cutting inside onto his stronger left foot to shoot or play through balls
weaknesses: Lower defensive workload, limited tracking back
Injury-management concerns given heavy minutes at a young age
Can occasionally over-rely on individual dribbles rather than quick combination play
tactical_notes: Thrives in a possession-based 4-3-3 as an inverted right winger, staying high and wide to stretch the back line before drifting infield to combine with central midfielders and overlap with the full-back.
role_fit: Best suited to systems built around positional play with overlapping full-backs and a goal-side winger who can isolate defenders 1v1.
risk_notes: Workload management is the main risk - clubs should monitor minutes given his age and the physical demands of his playing style.

profile_summary: A two-footed wide forward who blends consistent goal output with high-volume defensive pressing, making him one of the most complete attacking wingers in the Premier League.
strengths: Reliable goal and assist numbers from the right flank
Strong pressing intensity and willingness to track back
Comfortable cutting inside or going outside depending on the full-back's positioning
Set-piece delivery and end product from wide areas
weaknesses: History of recurring muscular injuries from heavy workload
Can become isolated in teams without overlapping full-back support
Occasionally tries to do too much individually in the final third
tactical_notes: Operates as a right-sided forward in a front three within a high-press 4-3-3, combining with an overlapping full-back and inverted central midfielders to overload the half-space.
role_fit: Fits high-intensity, possession-heavy systems that demand two-way wide forwards capable of both creating and pressing.
risk_notes: Injury history is the primary risk factor; rotation policy and squad depth should be considered when signing.

profile_summary: An elite left-sided forward whose blend of explosive pace, finishing and chance creation makes him a focal point of any transition-based attack.
strengths: Outstanding output in both goals and assists
Devastating in transition due to acceleration and dribbling at speed
Strong left-footed finishing from wide and central positions
Big-game performance record in knockout football
weaknesses: Defensive contribution remains limited
Can be drawn into confrontations and pick up bookings
Effectiveness drops in low-block, low-space games
tactical_notes: Best deployed as a left-sided forward in a counter-attacking or transition-heavy 4-3-3, given space to run in behind a high defensive line.
role_fit: Ideal for teams that win the ball in midfield and look to attack quickly through wide channels.
risk_notes: Disciplinary record (yellow/red cards) is worth monitoring for suspension risk in tight fixture schedules.

profile_summary: A versatile right-sided or central attacker known for intelligent movement off the ball and clinical finishing in big moments.
strengths: Versatile across multiple forward positions
Strong movement to find pockets of space between lines
Composed finisher with both feet
Good combination play in tight areas
weaknesses: Lower defensive pressing numbers compared to peers
Can be inconsistent in extended run of games
Sometimes overshadowed positionally by more dominant wide players
tactical_notes: Effective as a right-sided forward who can drift into a false-nine role, useful for teams wanting positional rotation in the front line.
role_fit: Suits systems with fluid front-three rotations and central overloads.
risk_notes: Squad rotation and minutes management are the main considerations rather than performance risk.

profile_summary: A powerful left-footed winger whose ball-carrying ability from deep positions creates significant transition threat.
strengths: Elite progressive carrying numbers, breaking lines with the ball at his feet
Direct dribbling threat in 1v1 situations
Physical profile suited to duels and holding off defenders
Good end product when running at retreating defences
weaknesses: Decision-making in the final pass can be inconsistent
Lower involvement in build-up phases
Pressing intensity below top-tier wingers
tactical_notes: Best used as a left winger in a counter-attacking system, dropping deep to receive and driving forward into space.
role_fit: Suits teams that defend in a mid-to-low block and look to transition quickly through wide carriers.
risk_notes: Consistency across a 38-game season has historically been a question mark.

profile_summary: A technically gifted left-footed winger who combines close control, unpredictability and a high creative output from the left flank.
strengths: Excellent dribbling in congested areas
Strong chance creation (xAG) and key pass numbers
Comfortable operating in both wide and half-space positions
Good pressing work-rate for a primary creator
weaknesses: Effectiveness can dip against deep, disciplined defensive blocks
Occasional tendency to hold onto the ball too long
Adaptation period typical when moving to a new league
tactical_notes: Operates as a left-sided forward who can also rotate into a free attacking-midfield role, well suited to a 4-2-3-1 or 4-3-3 with rotation.
role_fit: Fits possession-oriented teams that value individual creativity within a structured framework.
risk_notes: League-adaptation curve should be factored into short-term performance expectations after a transfer.

profile_summary: A right-footed wide player with an unusually balanced profile of dribbling, progressive passing and chance creation from the right flank.
strengths: High progressive pass output for a winger
Strong set-piece delivery and crossing range
Good dribbling success rate in wide areas
Versatile enough to play as a inverted winger or attacking midfielder
weaknesses: Injury history including muscular issues
Goal output lower than elite peers at his position
Adapting to a new league's physical intensity
tactical_notes: Suited to a right-wing role in a possession-based 4-2-3-1, providing width and delivery while supporting overlaps from a full-back.
role_fit: Fits teams that build attacks through combination play and crossing from wide areas.
risk_notes: Injury record warrants squad-depth planning at the wide-forward positions.

profile_summary: A physically dominant penalty-box striker with an extraordinary goals-per-90 record and elite finishing efficiency.
strengths: Outstanding xG and goal conversion rate
Powerful in aerial and physical duels
Clinical finishing with both feet
Constant penalty-area threat that occupies two centre-backs
weaknesses: Limited involvement in general build-up play
Lower chance-creation output for teammates
Can become isolated if service from wide areas is poor
tactical_notes: Best deployed as a central striker in a system that supplies high-quality service from wide areas and creative midfielders.
role_fit: Fits teams built around crossing, cutbacks and quick combination play into the box.
risk_notes: Heavily reliant on service quality - a drop in chance creation from teammates directly affects output.

profile_summary: A complete modern striker who combines clinical finishing with strong link-up play and pressing intensity.
strengths: High and consistent goal output
Good link-up play dropping into midfield
Strong pressing numbers for a forward
Composed finisher in one-on-one situations
weaknesses: Aerial threat slightly below elite target-man profiles
Can be quiet in matches with limited service
Occasional drop in efficiency against deep low blocks
tactical_notes: Fits as a lone striker or part of a front-two, dropping to link play while a partner attacks space behind.
role_fit: Suits possession teams that value a striker who can both finish and combine in build-up.
risk_notes: Minor injury niggles historically; squad rotation across congested periods recommended.

profile_summary: An explosive, athletic striker whose pace in behind and aerial power make him a constant in-behind and aerial threat.
strengths: Elite pace to attack space in behind defensive lines
Strong aerial presence for crosses and set pieces
High shot volume and conversion rate
Effective in transition-based attacking systems
weaknesses: Injury history affecting consistent availability
Limited involvement in deep build-up play
Can be marginalised against teams that defend with a low, compact block
tactical_notes: Best in a system that plays direct, vertical football with quick service in behind a high defensive line.
role_fit: Suits counter-attacking teams or those who dominate territory and create in-behind opportunities via through-balls.
risk_notes: Durability across a full season is the key risk factor for prospective buyers.

profile_summary: An elite goal-scoring forward whose combination of speed, finishing and big-match experience places him among the most dangerous attackers in the world.
strengths: World-class finishing and conversion rates
Elite acceleration and pace in transition
High chance creation output alongside goal output
Big-game and knockout-tournament performance record
weaknesses: Pressing contribution below some peers
Tactical fit can require system adjustments around his preferred central/left zones
High wage and market value impact on squad-building flexibility
tactical_notes: Effective as a central striker or left-sided forward in systems that prioritise verticality and 1v1 space for him to attack.
role_fit: Fits teams built to maximise individual brilliance in transition while maintaining defensive solidity elsewhere.
risk_notes: Squad-building and financial flexibility are the main practical considerations given his profile and value.

profile_summary: A complete striker renowned for combining elite finishing with exceptional deep-link play and chance creation for others.
strengths: Outstanding combination of goals and assists
Elite passing range including long-range progressive passes
Strong aerial ability and hold-up play
Leadership and experience in big matches
weaknesses: Mobility has gradually declined with age
Lower pressing intensity than younger forwards
Heavy reliance on him as a creative outlet can be exploited if marked tightly
tactical_notes: Fits as a deep-lying or traditional number 9 in a possession system that uses his passing range to dictate tempo from advanced areas.
role_fit: Suits teams wanting a striker who is also a creative hub, ideal for systems with fast wide forwards exploiting his passes.
risk_notes: Age-related physical decline should be factored into medium-to-long-term squad planning.

profile_summary: A veteran elite finisher whose movement and positioning inside the box remain exceptional despite advancing age.
strengths: Elite movement and finishing instincts in the box
Strong aerial threat on crosses and set pieces
Experience reading defensive structures to find space
Consistent goal output relative to minutes played
weaknesses: Reduced pressing range due to age
Pace to attack in behind has diminished
Physical recovery between matches requires careful management
tactical_notes: Best as a focal-point striker in a possession system that creates high-quality chances inside the box rather than relying on him to press or carry the ball.
role_fit: Suits teams with strong service from wide areas and central creators, minimising his defensive workload.
risk_notes: Age and recovery time should inform rotation policy across congested fixture periods.

profile_summary: A highly creative attacking midfielder whose close control and ability to operate between the lines make him difficult to mark.
strengths: Outstanding dribbling and close control in tight spaces
High chance creation (xAG) and shot-creating actions
Versatile across attacking-midfield and wide forward positions
Good progressive carrying to break defensive lines
weaknesses: Injury history including significant lower-body issues
Lower defensive pressing volume compared to some peers
Effectiveness can dip when forced wide rather than central
tactical_notes: Best utilised as a central attacking midfielder or inside forward in a possession system, given freedom to drift into pockets between the lines.
role_fit: Fits teams that build through central combination play and want a creative outlet between the lines.
risk_notes: Injury history is the key risk; careful workload management is recommended.

profile_summary: An elite creative midfielder with outstanding chance-creation numbers and a rare combination of vision, dribbling and pressing intensity.
strengths: Elite xAG and key-pass output among attacking midfielders
Strong progressive carrying and dribbling in central areas
High pressing intensity for a creative player
Versatile across number 10, left-wing and central-midfield roles
weaknesses: Adaptation period when moving to a new league and system
Goal output slightly behind his creative numbers
Can be a focal point for opposition man-marking schemes
tactical_notes: Best deployed centrally as a number 10 or left-sided attacking midfielder in a possession system that funnels play through him.
role_fit: Suits high-pressing, possession-dominant teams that want a creative hub who also contributes defensively.
risk_notes: Adaptation to a new league's physicality and tempo is the primary short-term consideration.

profile_summary: A technically excellent attacking midfielder capable of operating across multiple advanced positions with strong end product.
strengths: Versatile across attacking-midfield, wide and central roles
Strong shot and chance-creation numbers
Excellent close control in tight areas
Good final-third decision-making under pressure
weaknesses: Form can fluctuate across a season
Defensive pressing output below some peers
Best output tied closely to overall team rhythm
tactical_notes: Fits as an attacking midfielder or inside forward in a possession-dominant system with rotational movement in the final third.
role_fit: Suits teams with high possession share that create through combination play in tight central areas.
risk_notes: Performance consistency across a long season is the main consideration for squad planning.

profile_summary: A composed, high-IQ attacking midfielder whose passing range and movement make him the creative fulcrum of his team.
strengths: Elite progressive passing and chance creation
Excellent spatial awareness to find pockets between lines
Strong set-piece delivery
High pass completion percentage for an attacking player
weaknesses: Lower goal output relative to elite number 10s
Physical duels can be a challenge against more powerful midfielders
Effectiveness reduced if isolated from supporting runners
tactical_notes: Best as a number 10 or right-sided attacking midfielder in a possession system, dropping into midfield to dictate tempo.
role_fit: Fits teams built on patient possession and combination play in the final third.
risk_notes: Physical robustness against high-press, aggressive opponents is worth monitoring.

profile_summary: A technically elite central midfielder whose close control, passing range and tempo-setting ability define modern possession football.
strengths: Outstanding progressive passing output
Exceptional close control and press resistance
High pass completion percentage in tight spaces
Strong tactical intelligence to dictate match tempo
weaknesses: Injury history including recurring muscular issues
Lower direct goal/assist output than advanced attackers
Defensive ground-covering below holding-midfielder standards
tactical_notes: Best as a central midfielder in a possession-based 4-3-3, operating between the lines to receive, turn and progress play.
role_fit: Ideal for teams whose identity is built on retaining possession and progressing the ball through midfield combinations.
risk_notes: Recurring injury history is the primary risk factor and should inform medical due diligence.

profile_summary: A dynamic central midfielder combining box-to-box running, goal-scoring instincts and strong defensive engagement.
strengths: High goal output for a central midfielder
Strong progressive carrying and late box-arrival movement
Good tackling and defensive engagement numbers
Versatile across multiple midfield roles
weaknesses: High involvement can occasionally affect positional discipline
Disciplinary record (cards) worth monitoring
Workload management important given his all-action style
tactical_notes: Fits as an advanced central midfielder in a 4-3-3, combining late runs into the box with defensive support in transition.
role_fit: Suits teams wanting a goal-scoring midfielder who also contributes defensively in a balanced midfield three.
risk_notes: Workload and disciplinary record should be monitored across a demanding fixture calendar.

profile_summary: An energetic, press-resistant central midfielder whose intensity and ball-retention skills make him a key tempo-setter.
strengths: High pressing intensity and ball-recovery numbers
Strong press resistance in tight areas
Good progressive carrying for a central midfielder
High work-rate across both phases of play
weaknesses: Injury history including significant knee issues
Decision-making in the final third still developing
Can be drawn out of position by his intensity
tactical_notes: Best as a central or left-sided midfielder in a high-press possession system that values intensity and ball retention.
role_fit: Fits teams whose midfield identity is built on pressing triggers and quick ball recovery.
risk_notes: Long-term injury management following past knee surgery is an important consideration.

profile_summary: A versatile, athletic midfielder capable of playing across the midfield three with strong defensive engagement and ball-carrying ability.
strengths: High tackling and interception numbers
Strong progressive carrying to escape pressure
Versatile across defensive and central midfield roles
Good agility to cover ground laterally
weaknesses: Final-third output remains limited
Positional discipline can vary depending on role
Passing range less expansive than elite deep-lying playmakers
tactical_notes: Fits as a left-sided central or defensive midfielder in a system requiring athletic cover and ball progression under pressure.
role_fit: Suits teams that need a dynamic, two-way midfielder capable of covering multiple roles across a season.
risk_notes: Role flexibility is a strength, but a settled long-term position may maximise development.

profile_summary: A defensively dominant central midfielder with excellent ball-recovery numbers and increasingly influential passing range.
strengths: Elite tackling and interception output
Strong aerial duel success in midfield
Good progressive passing to start attacks from deep
Leadership and positional discipline
weaknesses: Goal/assist output still below elite attacking midfielders
Pace can be tested against the quickest transition players
Primarily effective in settled, structured systems
tactical_notes: Best as a holding or box-to-box midfielder in a 4-3-3, screening the back line while progressing the ball into midfield.
role_fit: Fits possession-based teams that need defensive solidity without sacrificing the ability to build play from deep.
risk_notes: Few significant risk factors; primarily a positional and tactical-fit consideration.

profile_summary: An elite deep-lying midfielder whose tactical intelligence, passing range and tempo control underpin his team's entire structure.
strengths: Outstanding progressive passing and tempo control
Excellent positional sense to screen the back line
Strong aerial and tackling numbers for a deep midfielder
High pass completion percentage under pressure
weaknesses: Mobility to cover wide areas is moderate compared to younger peers
Heavy tactical reliance on him can be exploited if man-marked
Recovery from injuries can affect squad rhythm given his importance
tactical_notes: Best as the single pivot in a possession-based 4-3-3 or 3-2-4-1, dictating tempo and distribution from deep.
role_fit: Fits teams whose entire structure depends on a controlling deep-lying midfielder.
risk_notes: Given his outsized tactical importance, injury absence has historically had a significant team-level impact.

profile_summary: A physically imposing defensive midfielder whose positioning and ball-winning ability provide a defensive platform for more progressive teammates.
strengths: Strong tackling and interception numbers
Good aerial presence in midfield duels
Reliable positional discipline screening the back line
Composed under pressure when playing out from the back
weaknesses: Progressive passing range below elite deep-lying playmakers
Final-third involvement minimal
Can be exposed in matches requiring more proactive ball-carrying
tactical_notes: Best as a defensive midfielder in a double pivot, providing defensive cover alongside a more progressive partner.
role_fit: Suits teams that prioritise defensive solidity and structured build-up over individual progression from midfield.
risk_notes: Primarily a tactical-fit consideration rather than a risk factor.

profile_summary: An experienced, versatile midfielder whose passing range and game intelligence allow him to control matches from deep or wide positions.
strengths: Elite progressive passing and assist output
High pass completion percentage under pressure
Versatile across defensive midfield and right-back roles
Strong set-piece delivery
weaknesses: Defensive ground-coverage has declined slightly with age
Pace can be tested by quick wide forwards when played at full-back
Effectiveness tied to team's overall possession share
tactical_notes: Best as a deep-lying midfielder in a possession system, dictating tempo and distribution similar to a quarterback role.
role_fit: Fits teams built around sustained possession and progressive passing from deep positions.
risk_notes: Age-related considerations apply to long-term squad planning rather than immediate performance.

profile_summary: An attack-minded full-back whose passing range from deep makes him one of the most creative defenders in the game.
strengths: Elite progressive passing and assist numbers from full-back
Exceptional set-piece delivery
Strong ability to invert into midfield and dictate play
High chance creation output for a defender
weaknesses: Defensive positioning and recovery pace have drawn scrutiny against quick wide forwards
Effectiveness reduced in deep defensive blocks without ball progression duties
Tactical adaptation required in new systems
tactical_notes: Best as a right-back in a possession system that allows him to invert into a midfield passing role during build-up.
role_fit: Fits teams that build attacks through their full-backs and want defensive players who function as auxiliary playmakers.
risk_notes: Defensive solidity in transition is the main consideration when assessing tactical fit.

profile_summary: An explosive left-back whose pace and progressive carrying ability provide constant width and transition threat.
strengths: Elite pace for recovery runs and overlapping attacks
Strong progressive carrying numbers from left-back
Good 1v1 dribbling success in wide areas
High work-rate covering the full width of the pitch
weaknesses: Defensive positioning can be inconsistent when caught high upfield
Crossing and final-ball accuracy room for improvement
Injury history including significant lower-body issues
tactical_notes: Best as a left-back in a high-line possession system, overlapping with a winger to create 2v1 situations on the flank.
role_fit: Fits teams that play with an aggressive defensive line and want width provided by attacking full-backs.
risk_notes: Injury history (including ACL) means medical assessment and gradual workload increases are recommended.

profile_summary: A powerful, attack-minded left-back combining physicality with strong progressive carrying and goal-scoring ability for a defender.
strengths: High progressive carrying output from deep positions
Strong physical profile in duels
Good goal-scoring record for a full-back
Effective overlapping and underlapping runs
weaknesses: Defensive positioning discipline can be inconsistent
Disciplinary record (cards) worth monitoring
Recovery pace can be tested in transition
tactical_notes: Best as a left-back in a system with a back three or back four that encourages attacking overlaps and underlaps.
role_fit: Fits teams that want physically dominant, attack-minded full-backs contributing directly to goal output.
risk_notes: Disciplinary record and defensive discipline are the main considerations for tactical fit.

profile_summary: An athletic, two-way right-back whose pace and crossing ability make him a constant outlet on the right flank.
strengths: Elite pace for both attacking and defensive transitions
Strong progressive carrying and crossing numbers
Good defensive recovery speed
Consistent goal and assist contributions for a full-back
weaknesses: Defensive positioning in central areas can be tested
1v1 defending against technical wingers occasionally inconsistent
Effectiveness reduced in deep, narrow defensive systems
tactical_notes: Best as a right-back (or wing-back) in a system that uses width and transitions, providing an outlet on both sides of the ball.
role_fit: Fits teams playing with a back three/wing-back system or an attacking back four that exploits the flanks.
risk_notes: Primarily a tactical-fit and positional consideration rather than a major risk factor.

profile_summary: A composed, physically dominant centre-back whose positioning and ball-playing ability anchor a high defensive line.
strengths: Excellent positioning and aerial duel success
Strong recovery pace covering behind a high line
Reliable passing under pressure to start build-up
Composed in 1v1 defending situations
weaknesses: Goal/assist output minimal as expected for the position
Occasional lapses in concentration on transitions
Limited ball-carrying into midfield compared to modern progressive centre-backs
tactical_notes: Best as a centre-back in a high-defensive-line system that requires recovery pace and aerial dominance.
role_fit: Fits possession-based teams that play out from the back and press high up the pitch.
risk_notes: Few significant risk factors; a low-risk, high-floor defensive profile.

profile_summary: An experienced, commanding centre-back whose reading of the game and aerial dominance provide defensive leadership.
strengths: Elite aerial duel success and positioning
Strong passing range to launch attacks from the back
Leadership and communication organising the defensive line
Composed under pressure in possession
weaknesses: Recovery pace has declined with age
Effectiveness reduced against very high-speed transition attacks
Squad rotation important given his age profile
tactical_notes: Best as a centre-back in a mid-to-high defensive line, using positioning and reading of the game to compensate for reduced pace.
role_fit: Fits possession-based teams that value distribution from the back and defensive organisation over raw recovery speed.
risk_notes: Age-related decline in pace should be considered for long-term squad planning.

profile_summary: An aggressive, physically dominant centre-back known for his duels, leadership and willingness to step into midfield.
strengths: Strong tackling and aerial duel numbers
Aggressive ball-winning approach stepping out of the defensive line
Experience in high-pressure knockout matches
Vocal leadership organising the back line
weaknesses: Disciplinary record (cards) worth monitoring
Aggressive approach can occasionally be exploited by quick forwards
Age-related rotation considerations
tactical_notes: Best as a centre-back in a possession system that benefits from a defender willing to press into midfield to win the ball back.
role_fit: Fits teams that play an aggressive, front-foot defensive style with a high line.
risk_notes: Disciplinary record and age-related rotation are the main considerations.

profile_summary: A mobile, ball-playing centre-back whose recovery pace and passing range suit modern high-line defensive systems.
strengths: Strong recovery pace for a centre-back
Good passing range to progress play from the back
Solid aerial and tackling numbers
Comfortable in both back-three and back-four systems
weaknesses: Occasional lapses in concentration leading to risky situations
Decision-making under high pressure can be inconsistent
Discipline in tackling situations worth monitoring
tactical_notes: Best as a centre-back in a high-press, high-line system that values recovery pace and distribution from the back.
role_fit: Fits possession-dominant teams that defend with an aggressive line and build out from the back.
risk_notes: Primarily a tactical-fit consideration; concentration lapses are the main on-pitch risk.
